title: Week roll-up matrix — availability + preferences per fixture
description: The core data surface of the dashboard. For the selected scope and week, show an availability matrix (rows = eligible players, cols = fixtures that week, cells = availability state). Cell hover/tap reveals the player's preference summary (WI-098 partial). Gives captains the big-picture view to make lineup decisions from data rather than cascading through screens.
acceptance_criteria: For the selected scope and week, the page renders a matrix: players down rows, fixtures across columns
Each cell shows availability state: available (green) / unavailable (red) / unknown (grey) with count-summary row+column
Cell hover / tap reveals the player's preference summary (WI-098 partial) in a popover
Each row shows both player initials AND full name — admin auth means full names are ok here
Eligible players = those assigned to teams within the selected scope for the active season
When scope is 'All Teams', large matrices paginate or virtualise rows beyond a documented threshold
Empty weeks (no fixtures in scope) show a friendly empty state
Sort by: availability score, name, preference match (used by WI-104 filter panel)
Matrix re-renders via HTMX when scope or week changes

performance_notes: Single-query join: fixtures × eligible_players × player_availability for the week
Preference data loaded lazily on hover/tap to keep initial render fast

testing_requirements: Integration test: matrix for a known week returns the expected shape

context.user_story: As a captain planning week N, I want one glanceable view of who's available for which fixture and what their playing style is — so I can stop cascading through separate screens.
